textDisplay:
They had a trailer for this movie in which none of the characters talked, and I was so excited for an animated movie with no voice acting, and all you would have would be the sounds of animals and machines and just nature in general, as well as possibly an epic score, and the only story telling would be through the animation itself.

I remember specifically telling my mom that “this is what an animated film with silent characters should look like”

Then a few weeks later we go to the theaters again and see another trailer for it however this time both the robots and the animals spoke, which 

A. makes little sense as how is a robot talking to a fox. yeah, animals talking to animals, I can suspend my disbelief for that cause they already effectively have their own languages. Animals who can speak English, alright fine, they can talk to people. Animals talking to robots without any humans present? Something just feels off about that. Is the robot speaking fox or is the fox speaking people and the robot also speaks people .

B. Delivered the same old sappy storyline we’ve been given 100 times over of belonging and friendship which could have still been done, and done in a more interesting way if they just didn’t speak.
textOriginal:
They had a trailer for this movie in which none of the characters talked, and I was so excited for an animated movie with no voice acting, and all you would have would be the sounds of animals and machines and just nature in general, as well as possibly an epic score, and the only story telling would be through the animation itself.

I remember specifically telling my mom that “this is what an animated film with silent characters should look like”

Then a few weeks later we go to the theaters again and see another trailer for it however this time both the robots and the animals spoke, which 

A. makes little sense as how is a robot talking to a fox. yeah, animals talking to animals, I can suspend my disbelief for that cause they already effectively have their own languages. Animals who can speak English, alright fine, they can talk to people. Animals talking to robots without any humans present? Something just feels off about that. Is the robot speaking fox or is the fox speaking people and the robot also speaks people .

B. Delivered the same old sappy storyline we’ve been given 100 times over of belonging and friendship which could have still been done, and done in a more interesting way if they just didn’t speak.
